mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for return difference fails to reach significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with stable consumer price inflation indicate a healthy demand environment without overheating, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative returns in greater than 50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during post-shock recovery phases reflects strong capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in greater than 55% of recovery period instances or loses predictive power when filtered by regime classification in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 50% during sustained payroll expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of triggered instances or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation outpacing the federal funds rate indicates negative real rates and impending monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction effect yields positive returns in greater than 55% of cases or loses predictive accuracy when real rates turn positive in subsequent months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3 percent during late-cycle expansions confirms sustained aggregate demand and earnings resilience, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Signal generates negative returns in greater than 60% of late-cycle instances or fails to outperform a zero-alpha benchmark in walk-forward tests over 3-year horizons.
